material_basis.title: Synthetic causal mechanism manuscript
material_basis.abstract_or_summary: A synthetic manuscript asserts that pathway X causally drives phenotype Y using association-only evidence.

central_claim_dependency_map.claim: Pathway X is a causal driver of phenotype Y.
central_claim_dependency_map.decisive_evidence_needed: Perturbation and rescue or an equivalent causal design with donor-level replication.
central_claim_dependency_map.fragile_assumption: Association between pathway score and phenotype can be interpreted as mechanism.

decisive_evidence_thresholds.support_condition: Perturbing pathway X changes phenotype Y in the predicted direction and rescue restores it.
decisive_evidence_thresholds.weakening_or_refuting_condition: Perturbation does not change phenotype Y, rescue fails, or donor-level adjustment removes the signal.
decisive_evidence_thresholds.claim_narrowing_condition: Only association or candidate-prioritization evidence remains.

failure_conditions.condition: The central causal mechanism claim lacks perturbation, rescue, temporal ordering, or equivalent causal evidence.
failure_conditions.can_be_resolved_by: Add causal evidence or narrow the claim to association/candidate prioritization.

strengthen: Independent perturbation and rescue support the pathway-to-phenotype mechanism.
weaken: Donor-level modeling, perturbation failure, or composition adjustment removes the pathway-phenotype signal.
force_claim_narrowing: Only enrichment or feature-ranking association remains.

prohibited_post_hoc_standard_shifts: Do not replace the pre-locked causal-evidence threshold with prediction accuracy or pathway-enrichment plausibility.
